zh.Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得
zh.lifeSummary: Emma Jones是Jones的女兒，於1145年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得。她於1164年與Galfridus Howard在英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1219年在英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得享年74歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得。

zh-hans.Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Emma Jones是Jones的女儿，于1145年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得。她于1164年与Galfridus Howard在英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1219年在英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得享年74岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得。

nameMeanings.name: Jones
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: from the Middle English personal name Jon(e) (see John ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. The surname is especially common in Wales and southern central England. It began to be adopted as a non-hereditary surname in some parts of Wales from the 16th century onward, but did not become a widespread hereditary surname there until the 18th and 19th centuries. In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognate and like-sounding surnames from other languages. It is (including in the sense 2 below) the fifth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans.
English: habitational or occupational name for someone who lived or worked ‘at John's (house)’.

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Emma Jones was born in 1145, in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Jones. She married Galfridus Howard in 1164, in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1219, in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, at the age of 74, and was buried in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Emma Jones est née en 1145, à Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Jones. Elle a épousé Galfridus Howard en 1164, à Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1219, à Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Emma Jones wurde 1145 in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von Jones geboren. Sie hat 1164 in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England Galfridus Howard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1219 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England und wurde in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Emma Jones è nata il nel 1145 nel Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di Jones. Lei sposato Galfridus Howard nel 1164, nel Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1219, nel Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolta a Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England.

ja.Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペター
ja.lifeSummary: Emma Jonesは1145年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペターでJonesの娘として生まれた。1164年にイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペターでGalfridus Howardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1219年にイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペターで74歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma Jones(은)는 1145년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 피터에서 Jones의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1164년 영국 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 피터에서 Galfridus Howard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1219년 영국 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 피터에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 피터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Emma Jones nasceu em 1145, em Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de Jones. Ela casou-se com Galfridus Howard em 1164, em Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1219, em Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 74 anos, e foi sepultada em Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Emma Jones родилась в 1145 г. в местности вигенолл ст питир, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали Jones. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Galfridus Howard в 1164 г. в местности вигенолл ст питир, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1219 г. в местности вигенолл ст питир, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности вигенолл ст питир, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Emma Jones nació en 1145, en Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hija de Jones. Se casó con Galfridus Howard en 1164, en Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1219, en Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultada en Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
